title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Inspector Japp and Poirot discuss the possibility that Mr. Morley may have been a pawn in a larger scheme involving Alistair Blunt. Meanwhile, Alfred, an employee who has worked for Mr. Morley for two weeks, provides a detailed account of the visitors at the dental practice on the morning of Morley's death. Although Alfred feels insecure due to past mistakes, he is eager to assist as a witness.

Alfred describes a sequence of visitors: a lady with a young girl, a Mrs. Soper looking for a Mr. Moore, a society lady in a Daimler, a tall soldier, Inspector Japp, and an American gentleman. The American arrived early for an eleven-thirty appointment but left before seeing the doctor because Morley did not ring the bell until approximately eleven-forty. Following this, a man named Mr. Brent arrived in a Rolls-Royce. After Mr. Brent departed, Miss Seymour Beryl Chilwell arrived, and Alfred escorted her up in the elevator. Subsequently, two men arrived: a short man with a high-pitched voice and a fat foreigner named Mr. Ambrosio. Alfred escorted Mr. Ambrosio to see Mr. Morley but did not see him leave.

After twelve o'clock, Alfred sat in the elevator reading a detective novel. He notes that a lady named Miss Shady arrived and waited until one o'clock, becoming angry when Morley failed to ring the bell. Alfred also mentions that around noon, Mr. Neville's young boyfriend arrived looking for Miss Neville; he entered the waiting room but then disappeared. Poirot suggests that discussing the murder with Alfred might help jog his memory, though Japp expresses concern regarding this approach.
